subject: Fix mongodb errors on application startup
commit_message:
Fix mongodb errors on application startup

Revert Dockerfile refactor due to permissions issues

Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-2747
Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-2590
Signed-off-by: Kai Lu <lukai@chinamobile.com>
Change-Id: I84075575cc75090876416cb13e0ac197a8e0f6e3
Signed-off-by: Remigiusz Janeczek <remigiusz.janeczek@nokia.com>

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

(4 comments)

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

Ok so I looked into the issue (not sure about the solution, if you could check it, then it would be nice), it looks like MongoDB AutoConfiguration doesn't run at all.

It seems that there were some changes in underlying Mongo dependencies, some classes were moved maybe.

Looking at MongoAutoConfiguration class: https://docs.spring.io/spring-boot/docs/current/api/org/springframework/boot/autoconfigure/mongo/MongoAutoConfiguration.html

There is:
 @ConditionalOnClass(value=com.mongodb.client.MongoClient.class)

but the MongoClient.class is not available in any dependencies. To fix that we need:

         <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-data-mongodb</artifactId>
        </dependency>

in dcae-analytics-web pom file.

Didn't verify it yet, but it seems as the most probable solution. Also using the spring-boot-starter-data-mongodb we could maybe remove some other mongodb dependencies.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

I also checked it. 
In project exists both spring and spring-boot dependencies, so it is difficult to investigate which dependency introduce that problem. 

It should be resolved by using compliant version of libraries imported by spring/spring-boot dependencies and fix MogodbAutoConfiguration. Probably application requires also implementation of MongoDataBaseFactory.

Unable to build due to a non-resolvable parent POM. Search logs for "[FATAL] Non-resolvable parent POM" to find the logs.

The fix for this is usually to make sure your poms are pointing to parents that exist. Double check:

 * groupId is correct and no typos
 * artifactId is correct and no typos
 * version is correct for branch ( https://jenkins.onap.org/job/dcaegen2-analytics-tca-gen2-master-verify-java/194/ )

The container started with the current dockerfile. Not sure what was the exact issue, I tried changing the permissions etc and I was able to manually create files in the /opt/logs/dcae-analytics-tca/ while the app couldn't, don't know why (maybe Alexander knows better). In the end I reverted the ordering of commands in dockerfile (keeping the integration image), which seems to work.

The source of the error seems to be MrSubscriberPollingAdvice class, line 107:
 final HttpStatus httpStatus = HttpStatus.resolve(Integer.parseInt(httpStatusCode.toString()));

seems that currently httpStatusCode.toString() returns: "200 OK" instead of "200" after changing it to:
 final HttpStatus httpStatus = HttpStatus.resolve(Integer.parseInt(httpStatusCode.toString().split(" ")[0]));

I didn't get that exception, but got a timeout from aai, I think it may be an issue with my lab/environment.

Patch Set 6:

(4 comments)

> Patch Set 6:
> 
> The container started with the current dockerfile. Not sure what was the exact issue, I tried changing the permissions etc and I was able to manually create files in the /opt/logs/dcae-analytics-tca/ while the app couldn't, don't know why (maybe Alexander knows better). In the end I reverted the ordering of commands in dockerfile (keeping the integration image), which seems to work.

Could you try following changes on the Dockerfile?
(diff from curr master not this commit)
diff --git a/dcae-analytics/dcae-analytics-tca-web/Dockerfile b/dcae-analytics/dcae-analytics-tca-web/Dockerfile
index 2791e03..ce50ca2 100644
--- a/dcae-analytics/dcae-analytics-tca-web/Dockerfile
+++ b/dcae-analytics/dcae-analytics-tca-web/Dockerfile
@@ -18,12 +18,12 @@ USER root
 #Symlink to overcome Docker limitation to put ARG inside ENTRYPOINT
 RUN mkdir -p /var/log/ONAP/dcaegen2/analytics/tca-gen2 && \
     addgroup $group && adduser --system --disabled-password --no-create-home --ingroup $group $user && \
-    chmod g+rwx /opt; \
-    chmod u+rw /var/log/ONAP/dcaegen2/analytics/tca-gen2 && \
-    chown -R $user /opt && \
-    chown -R $user /var/log/ONAP/dcaegen2/analytics/tca-gen2 && \
+    chmod u+rwx /opt; \
+    chmod -R u+rwx /var/log/ONAP/ && \
+    chown -R $user:$group /opt && \
+    chown -R $user:$group /var/log/ONAP/ && \
     ln -s ${FINAL_JAR} tca-gen2.jar && \
-    chown $user ${FINAL_JAR} tca-gen2.jar
+    chown $user:$group ${FINAL_JAR} tca-gen2.jar
 
 EXPOSE 8100
